title: Acantholimon parviflorum
intro: Acantholimon parviflorum, also known as Dwarf Featherbush, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Plumbaginaceae family, native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly to treat digestive issues and respiratory problems, due to its health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The bioactive constituents of Acantholimon parviflorum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Acantholimon parviflorum is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for skin issues.
taxonomy: Acantholimon parviflorum is a species of flowering plant in the family Plumbaginaceae. It belongs to the genus Acantholimon and is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Iran and Turkey. The classification of Acantholimon parviflorum also includes its rank as a species, which is a taxonomic rank below the genus level. The plant's classification is also reflected in its binomial nomenclature, with the species name parviflorum indicating its small flowers.
